title: equisetidae
intro: The rank Equisetidae is a subclass of vascular plants that includes living and extinct species. It is a taxonomic group characterized by its unique, hollow, and jointed stems. The Equisetidae subclass is a distinct and separate group from other subclasses of vascular plants, and it includes the Equisetum genus, among others. The Equisetidae rank contains orders such as Equisetales, and other extinct orders, but not asparagales, malpighiales, or poales, which belong to different subclasses.

orders.order_desc: Bryales is an order of mosses within the class Bryopsida, subclass Bryidae, and division Bryophyta.
